notes.total: 148 tickets scoped across 6 sprints is a healthy pipeline, indicating sustained delivery capacity throughout the quarter.
notes.completed: 131 of 148 tickets completed demonstrates strong execution against quarter commitments.
notes.completion_rate: 89% completion rate exceeds healthy threshold and reflects disciplined sprint planning and execution.
notes.releases_shipped: 13 releases shipped across 6 sprints (median 1 per sprint) indicates a consistent delivery cadence with some sprints shipping multiple releases.
notes.oos_total: 36 out-of-sprint tickets (24% of total) indicates reactive work or sprint planning issues — flag for review to improve sprint discipline.
notes.oos_open: Zero open OOS items — all unplanned work was resolved by quarter end.
notes.type_split: 18% bug ratio (27 bugs) is healthy and indicates quality is under control; majority of work focused on stories and tasks.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 1 release per sprint shows consistent shipping; mean of 2.2 indicates some sprints batched multiple releases.
notes.time_logged: 463 hours logged against 1,982 estimated is significantly under-tracked (23% accuracy) — time logging discipline needs attention.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 77% variance between estimates and actuals indicates poor estimation accuracy; most tickets were significantly over-estimated or not logged correctly.
notes.no_estimate: 23 tickets (16%) completed without estimates is within acceptable range but could be improved for better planning reliability.
notes.rollover: 37 tickets (25%) rolled over across sprints signals sprint commitment issues — review sprint sizing and capacity planning.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 36 days is high; mean of 57.6 days indicates some tickets spent months in progress — flag blockers and improve flow.
notes.assignee_workload: Workload heavily concentrated on Riccardo Fachin (42 tickets, non-team) and Dejan Kosanovic (35 tickets); Vitomir Vranic shows only 54% completion rate requiring follow-up.
